report_name: Carnival Data Breach Exposed Nearly 6 Million After Social Engineering Attack

threat_actors.name: ShinyHunters
threat_actors.description: Extortion group suggested by security researchers to be possibly linked to the Carnival Corporation data breach. Known for targeting large corporations and leaking data.
threat_actors.goals: Data theft for the purpose of extortion.

attack_patterns.name: Phishing
attack_patterns.description: The threat actor used a social engineering attack to deceive a Carnival employee, likely involving a sophisticated pretext, to gain initial access to the company's IT systems.

attack_patterns.name: Valid Accounts
attack_patterns.description: Once inside the network, the attacker used the compromised employee account to perform reconnaissance and move laterally within the IT environment.

attack_patterns.name: Data from Information Repositories
attack_patterns.description: The threat actor located and aggregated sensitive customer and employee data from various internal systems and data repositories.

attack_patterns.name: Exfiltration Over C2 Channel
attack_patterns.description: The attacker copied and transferred the personal identifiable information of nearly 6 million individuals from Carnival's network to an external, actor-controlled location.

identities.name: Carnival Corporation
identities.description: The world's largest cruise operator, which suffered a data breach originating from a social engineering attack that exposed the personal information of nearly 6 million customers and staff.

identities.name: Maine Attorney General
identities.description: The government office where Carnival Corporation filed a data breach notification, disclosing the number of individuals affected by the security incident.

indicators.name: Potential PowerShell Data Exfiltration Activity
indicators.pattern:
title: Potential PowerShell Data Exfiltration Activity
id: 7e8b1c2a-9d0f-4e3a-8b1c-2a9d0f4e3a8b
status: experimental
description: >
  Detects the use of PowerShell commands often associated with data staging and exfiltration, such as compressing archives or copying files to remote sessions. This was suggested as a hunting hint in the Carnival data breach investigation.
references:
  - https://www.wcnc.com/article/news/nation-world/carnival-data-breach-social-engineering-hack/275-c089a87d-30f1-4b10-85bb-650212ce9251
author: cyber.netsecops.io
date: 2026-06-01
tags:
  - attack.t1041
  - attack.exfiltration
  - attack.t1560.001
  - attack.collection
level: medium
logsource:
  category: process_creation
  product: windows
detection:
  selection:
    Image|endswith: '\powershell.exe'
    CommandLine|contains:
      - ' -enc '
      - 'Compress-Archive'
      - 'Copy-Item -ToSession'
  condition: selection
indicators.description: Detects PowerShell commands used for data compression and exfiltration, based on hunting hints provided in the analysis of the Carnival breach.
